name: Steel
description: An embeddable and extensible Scheme dialect built in Rust with R5RS compliance

implementation.comment: 75.5% Rust, 21.5% Scheme, 2.9% Racket. No C/C++ dependencies

language-family.comment: Scheme dialect with Racket-inspired module system (require/provide)

first-release.comment: GitHub repo created Feb 2020; first crates.io release (0.2.0) July 2023

typing.comment: Dynamic typing as is standard for Scheme

paradigm.comment: Functional at core like Scheme, but supports imperative style with set!

first-class-functions.comment: Functions are first-class as in all Scheme dialects

pattern-matching.comment: syntax-rules and syntax-case macros provide pattern matching

error-handling.comment: Scheme-style exceptions; Rust Result/Option auto-unwrap through VM

async-await.comment: Engine is not Send or Sync; single-threaded execution

iterators-generators.comment: Built-in list operations: map, filter, fold, etc.

module-system.comment: Racket-style modules with require and provide

standard-library.comment: Includes file I/O, process control, networking, math, string processing, data structures

compilation-model.comment: Runtime eval and experimental precompilation (compile command)

hot-reloading.comment: Modules can be reloaded; designed for embedding

repl-support.comment: Interactive REPL via steel-repl crate and CLI

debugging-support.comment: AST and bytecode inspection via CLI commands

startup-time.comment: Pure Rust bytecode VM; no JIT warmup

execution-speed.comment: Bytecode VM performance; R7RS benchmarks included but not published

garbage-collection.comment: Garbage collected with immutable data structures by default

api-safety.comment: Safe public API; unsafe contained internally

idiomatic-api.comment: RegisterFn trait for ergonomic function registration; automatic type conversions via FromSteelVal/IntoSteelVal

serde-integration.comment: serde dependency included for data serialization

async-compatible.comment: Engine is not Send or Sync; bound to single thread

function-registration.comment: RegisterFn trait and BuiltInModule for organizing functions

type-mapping.comment: Automatic via FromSteelVal/IntoSteelVal traits; Vec->list, HashMap->hashmap, Option->false, Result->unwrap

no-std-compatible.comment: Uses std for I/O, process control, and other features

wasm-compatible.comment: Online playground available at mattwparas.github.io demonstrating WASM support

blank-slate-mode.comment: Engine::new() includes standard primitives; no documented blank slate mode

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory

syscall-restrictions.comment: Standard library includes file I/O and process spawning by default

default-deny.comment: Comprehensive standard library available by default

call-script-from-rust.comment: Engine::run() executes Steel code and returns results

custom-types.comment: Rust structs can be registered via BuiltInModule

operator-overloading.comment: Scheme operators are functions; custom implementations possible

method-syntax.comment: Scheme uses prefix notation; no dot-notation methods

callbacks-events.comment: First-class functions can be stored and invoked as callbacks

crates-io-downloads.comment: Estimated based on ~470/month and first release July 2023

editor-support.comment: Language server available; used in Helix editor plugin system

example-projects.comment: Used as plugin system for Helix editor; steel-examples directory

documentation-quality.comment: Book available with embedding guide; API docs adequate but some gaps

maturity.comment: API relatively stable but may change while pre-1.0; R5RS mostly compliant

primary-use-cases.comment: Designed for embedding; used as Helix editor plugin system
